exam.name: Middle School Mathematics (5164)
exam.category_description: Exam-aligned prep for future educators with syllabus-mapped coverage built directly from official certification blueprints.
exam.hero_headline: Pass Praxis 5164 with middle school math mapped to the official objectives
exam.hero_subheadline: Study Number & Quantity through Statistics & Probability—including teaching scenarios—in focused 15-minute topics aligned to the ETS Study Companion.
exam.companion_problem: Most middle school math prep either feels like generic K–12 math review or ignores the instructional framing and scenario weighting that Praxis 5164 actually tests.
exam.companion_solution: We translate the official ETS syllabus into a full learning map with topic-sized notes, explicit domain boundaries, and consistent coverage of tasks of teaching mathematics.
exam.learner_profile: Aspiring middle school mathematics teacher preparing for selected-response and numeric-entry items, including substantial mathematics-in-teaching contexts. Comfortable with an on-screen graphing calculator; benefits from clear structure across numbers, algebra, functions, geometry, and statistics.

sections.title: Numbers and Operations
sections.description: Understanding operations and properties of the real number system, fractions, decimals, ratios, and number theory.

topics.title: Properties of Real Numbers
topics.description: Classifying real numbers and applying fundamental properties.
learning_objectives: Classify real numbers as natural, whole, integer, rational, or irrational.
Identify and apply properties of operations such as commutative, associative, and distributive.
Compare and order real numbers, including absolute values.

topics.title: Real Number Operations and Order of Operations
topics.description: Solving word problems and using the order of operations.
learning_objectives: Represent and solve word problems involving addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.
Use the order of operations to simplify computations and solve problems.
Determine the rationality of sums and products of rational and irrational numbers.

topics.title: Exponents, Radicals, and Scientific Notation
topics.description: Performing operations with integer exponents, radicals, and scientific notation.
learning_objectives: Perform operations involving integer exponents.
Approximate the value of radical expressions.
Use scientific notation to represent numbers, compare numbers, and perform calculations.

topics.title: Fractions, Decimals, and Percents
topics.description: Understanding relationships and converting among fractions, decimals, and percents.
learning_objectives: Convert among fractions, decimals, and percents.
Represent repeating decimals as fractions.
Use visual models to represent fractions, decimals, and percents.

topics.title: Ratios and Rates
topics.description: Using the language of ratio and rate to describe relationships.
learning_objectives: Use ratio language to describe relationships between two quantities.
Calculate unit rates and apply them to solve contextual problems.

topics.title: Proportional Relationships and Percents
topics.description: Identifying proportional relationships and solving percent problems.
learning_objectives: Identify and represent proportional relationships using scale factors and constant of proportionality.
Solve percent problems including discounts, markups, taxes, tips, simple interest, and percent error.

topics.title: Quantitative Reasoning and Dimensional Analysis
topics.description: Reasoning quantitatively and using units and dimensional analysis.
learning_objectives: Choose and interpret units consistently in formulas.
Choose and interpret the scale in graphs and data displays.
Solve problems involving dimensional analysis.

topics.title: Number Theory Concepts
topics.description: Using basic concepts of number theory to solve problems.
learning_objectives: Use the definitions of prime and composite numbers.
Solve problems involving factors, multiples, prime factorization, and divisibility.

sections.title: Algebra
sections.description: Creating, evaluating, and manipulating algebraic expressions, equations, and inequalities.

topics.title: Polynomial Operations
topics.description: Adding, subtracting, and multiplying linear and quadratic polynomials.
learning_objectives: Add, subtract, and multiply linear and quadratic polynomials.
Perform operations on polynomials with rational coefficients.

topics.title: Evaluating and Manipulating Expressions
topics.description: Working with expressions involving rational exponents and rewriting linear, quadratic, and exponential expressions.
learning_objectives: Evaluate, manipulate, and compare algebraic expressions involving rational exponents, radicals, and negative exponents.
Rewrite expressions to reveal properties of the quantity represented.
Interpret parts of expressions in terms of a real-world setting.

topics.title: Creating Equations and Inequalities
topics.description: Constructing equations from verbal descriptions and rearranging formulas.
learning_objectives: Translate verbal relationships into algebraic equations or expressions.
Use variables to construct and solve equations and inequalities in real-world contexts.
Rearrange formulas to solve for a specified variable.

topics.title: Recognizing Linear Relationships
topics.description: Determining and converting among various forms of linear equations.
learning_objectives: Determine the equation of a line from tables, graphs, or descriptions.
Convert among slope-intercept, point-slope, and standard forms of linear equations.
Extract information from linear equations presented in various forms.

topics.title: Solving Linear Equations and Inequalities
topics.description: Solving and justifying one-variable linear equations and inequalities.
learning_objectives: Solve one-variable linear equations and inequalities.
Represent solutions on a number line or in the xy-plane.
Justify each step in solving equations and inequalities.

topics.title: Solving Nonlinear Equations
topics.description: Finding solutions for absolute value and quadratic equations.
learning_objectives: Solve one-variable nonlinear equations, such as absolute value and quadratic equations.
Determine the nature of the solutions of a quadratic equation using the discriminant.
Write quadratic equations in factored form and interpret their graphs.

topics.title: Systems of Linear Equations
topics.description: Solving systems of two linear equations algebraically and graphically.
learning_objectives: Solve a system of two linear equations in two variables algebraically.
Solve a system of two linear equations graphically.
Find approximate solutions for systems containing linear, quadratic, or exponential functions.

topics.title: Graphing Inequalities and Systems
topics.description: Graphing systems of linear inequalities and interpreting solutions as viable or nonviable.
learning_objectives: Graph the solution set to a system of linear inequalities in two variables.
Represent constraints by systems of equations and inequalities.
Interpret solutions as viable or nonviable in a modeling context.

sections.title: Functions
sections.description: Identifying, defining, evaluating, and graphing linear, quadratic, and exponential functions.

topics.title: Identifying and Evaluating Functions
topics.description: Understanding whether a relation is a function and evaluating functions.
learning_objectives: Determine whether a given relation is a function.
Determine if a function presented algebraically, graphically, or in a table is linear, quadratic, or exponential.
Determine the value of a function for a specified value in its domain.

topics.title: Domain and Range
topics.description: Determining and interpreting the domain and range of a function.
learning_objectives: Determine the domain and range of a function from tables, graphs, or equations.
Interpret domain and range in real-world settings.

topics.title: Characteristics of Linear Functions
topics.description: Calculating intercepts and slopes of linear functions and interpreting points on graphs.
learning_objectives: Calculate intercepts of a line and interpret them in a modeling context.
Calculate and interpret the slope of a line from a table, equation, or graph.
Interpret the meaning of points on the graph of a proportional relationship, including the unit rate.

topics.title: Relationships Among Functions, Tables, and Graphs
topics.description: Determining equations from graphs and understanding function transformations.
learning_objectives: Determine an equation to represent a linear or quadratic function presented graphically.
Sketch a graph given an equation of a function, such as square root or absolute value.
Identify symbolic representations of functions created through horizontal/vertical translations or reflections.

topics.title: Analyzing Mathematical Models
topics.description: Using function notation, rate of change, and parameter interpretation.
learning_objectives: Interpret statements using function notation and parameters in terms of a context.
Calculate and interpret the rate of change of a function over a given interval.
Determine and interpret the x- and y-intercepts of quadratic functions.

topics.title: Comparing Function Types
topics.description: Differentiating between linear, quadratic, and exponential models.
learning_objectives: Identify situations in which a quantity changes at a constant rate per unit interval.
Identify situations where a quantity grows or decays by a constant percent rate per unit interval.
Observe that a quantity increasing exponentially eventually exceeds quantities increasing linearly or quadratically.

topics.title: Arithmetic Sequences
topics.description: Representing arithmetic sequences both recursively and explicitly.
learning_objectives: Write arithmetic sequences recursively.
Write arithmetic sequences with an explicit formula.
Model real-world situations using arithmetic sequences.

sections.title: Geometry and Measurement
sections.description: Understanding properties of geometric figures, transformations, coordinate geometry, and measurement systems.

topics.title: Lines and Angles
topics.description: Applying properties of parallel, perpendicular, and intersecting lines.
learning_objectives: Solve problems involving parallel, perpendicular, and intersecting lines.
Apply angle relationships, including supplementary, vertical, and alternate interior angles, to solve problems.

topics.title: Triangles and the Pythagorean Theorem
topics.description: Understanding properties of triangles and applying the Pythagorean theorem.
learning_objectives: Solve problems involving the Pythagorean theorem in two dimensions.
Identify characteristics of special triangles and use them to solve problems.
Determine if side lengths or angle measures produce unique, multiple, or no triangles using the triangle inequality theorem.

topics.title: Quadrilaterals and Other Polygons
topics.description: Identifying relationships among quadrilaterals and solving polygon problems.
learning_objectives: Identify the relationships among various quadrilaterals such as parallelograms, rectangles, and rhombuses.
Solve problems involving the sides and interior/exterior angles of regular polygons.

topics.title: Geometric Transformations
topics.description: Applying properties of translations, reflections, rotations, and dilations.
learning_objectives: Apply properties of rigid transformations and dilations to line segments, angles, and parallel lines.
Describe the sequence of transformations that maps a preimage onto an image.
Determine the coordinates of a point on an image following a given transformation.

topics.title: Congruence and Similarity
topics.description: Using concepts of congruence and similarity for 2D and 3D figures.
learning_objectives: Determine whether two geometric figures are congruent or similar.
Use congruence and similarity to solve problems involving two-dimensional and three-dimensional figures.

topics.title: Properties of Circles
topics.description: Solving problems relating to the circumference and area of a circle.
learning_objectives: Solve problems involving the circumference of a circle.
Solve problems involving the area of a circle.
Apply circle properties to solve composite area problems.

topics.title: Coordinate Geometry
topics.description: Interpreting relationships in the xy-plane using distance and midpoint.
learning_objectives: Use coordinate geometry to represent shapes and solve perimeter and area problems.
Determine the distance between two points in the coordinate plane.
Determine the midpoint of a line segment.

topics.title: Perimeter and Area of Polygons
topics.description: Calculating area and perimeter of polygons and composite figures.
learning_objectives: Calculate and interpret the perimeter and area of polygons composed of triangles and quadrilaterals.
Calculate changes in perimeter and area as polygon dimensions change.

topics.title: Surface Area and Volume of Solids
topics.description: Calculating surface area, volume, and using nets for 3D solids.
learning_objectives: Calculate surface area and volume of prisms, pyramids, cylinders, spheres, and composite solids.
Calculate changes in surface area and volume as the dimensions of a solid change.
Use two-dimensional nets to visualize and solve problems involving three-dimensional solids.

topics.title: Systems of Measurement
topics.description: Solving measurement and conversion problems in metric and US customary systems.
learning_objectives: Solve measurement, estimation, and conversion problems involving time, length, temperature, volume, and mass.
Use appropriate units of measurement in a given context.

sections.title: Statistics and Probability
sections.description: Interpreting data displays, calculating central tendency and dispersion, and evaluating probability models.

topics.title: Statistical Processes and Sampling
topics.description: Recognizing statistical questions and making population inferences.
learning_objectives: Recognize statistical questions that anticipate variability.
Distinguish between random and biased sampling.
Use statistics to make inferences about population parameters based on a sample.

topics.title: Data Displays and Relative Frequencies
topics.description: Representing and interpreting data using various charts and tables.
learning_objectives: Represent and analyze data in bar graphs, line graphs, circle graphs, boxplots, histograms, and scatterplots.
Calculate relative frequencies in two-way tables to describe possible associations.
Choose appropriate graphs based on whether data is numerical or categorical.

topics.title: Linear Models and Bivariate Data
topics.description: Using linear models to describe relationships between quantitative variables.
learning_objectives: Describe how two quantitative variables are related using association and correlation.
Use the equation of a linear model (line of best fit) to solve problems.
Interpret the slope and intercept of a linear model in the context of bivariate measurement data.

topics.title: Measures of Central Tendency and Dispersion
topics.description: Solving for and interpreting mean, median, mode, range, and interquartile range.
learning_objectives: Solve for the mean and weighted average of given sets of data.
Determine and interpret measures of center and spread in a variety of problems.
Describe the distribution of a set of data by its center and spread.

topics.title: Comparing Data Distributions
topics.description: Comparing the center and spread of different data sets and evaluating outliers.
learning_objectives: Use statistics appropriate to the shape of the data distribution to compare two or more data sets.
Interpret differences in center and spread, accounting for possible effects of outliers.
Summarize a given numerical data set in relation to its context.

topics.title: Counting Techniques and Sample Spaces
topics.description: Using counting principles and tree diagrams to determine outcomes.
learning_objectives: Use counting techniques, such as the fundamental counting principle, to answer questions.
Construct and interpret tree diagrams to model a finite sample space.

topics.title: Simple and Compound Probability
topics.description: Solving probability problems involving single and multiple events.
learning_objectives: Solve probability problems involving simple events.
Solve probability problems involving compound events, including independent and dependent events.

topics.title: Evaluating Probability Models
topics.description: Interpreting probability models and comparing theoretical to observed frequencies.
learning_objectives: Interpret a probability model (uniform and non-uniform) and use it to find probabilities of events.
Compare probabilities from a theoretical model to observed frequencies in generated data.
Identify possible sources of discrepancy if agreement between modeled and observed frequencies is not good.
